title: Attestplane Governance Ingestion Document (v1)
description: Opinionated subset of proof_bundle.schema.json optimised for AI governance platform consumption (Credo AI / Holistic AI / Modulos / Trustible / Saidot). Drops event payloads — keeps chain integrity proof, framework mappings, redaction policy, and optional Sigstore bundle. Goal: a governance dashboard can ingest one self-contained JSON document per audit window and surface 'evidence available' indicators against its existing obligation rows. Locked by the competitive_positioning_upgrade_plan_20260517 Track 4. Designed to be a strict subset of auditor_export.schema.json so any tool that consumes one can consume the other with field-level mapping.

ingestion_version.description: Frozen at 1 for the v1 ingestion shape.

producer.description: Identifies the Attestplane substrate instance that produced this document.

runtime_name.description: Free-form identifier of the runtime that emitted events (e.g., 'langsmith', 'langfuse', or a custom runtime name).

instance_id.description: Optional opaque identifier for the substrate instance; lets governance dashboards distinguish multiple Attestplane deployments.

chain_summary.$comment: Strict subset of auditor_export.chain_summary; same field names so the governance dashboard can use one mapping for both.

framework_coverage.description: Per-framework summary of obligation coverage. THIS is what governance dashboards read most: 'For framework X, obligations [a, b, c] have evidence; obligations [d, e] don't.' Surfacing absence is critical — a regulator audit needs to see what's missing, not just what's present.

implementation_status.description: The locked four-value enum per docs/policy/forbidden_claims.md § Obligation-registry implementation_status. Governance dashboards MUST surface this verbatim — translation to 'compliant' / 'certified' is prohibited by the claim-safety triad.

evidence_event_indexes.description: Indexes into the source proof bundle's events array. Provided for traceability; the governance ingestion document itself does NOT include the events.

anchor_summary.description: Optional: lightweight summary of any cryptographic anchors attached to the underlying chain. Governance dashboards typically surface 'anchored: yes/no' indicators; the full Rekor LogEntry / RFC-3161 TimeStampToken lives in the proof bundle, not here.

proof_bundle_uri.description: Optional pointer to where the governance dashboard can fetch the full proof bundle for deeper inspection. The substrate does NOT host this; deployers populate it with their own storage location (e.g., S3 / Azure Blob / on-prem).

legal_disclaimer.description: Plain-language statement that this document is a technical evidence summary, not a compliance opinion.
legal_disclaimer.default: This document is a technical evidence summary produced by an Attestplane substrate. It is not a compliance opinion. Consult qualified counsel for any regulatory determination. The implementation_status values follow the four-value enum defined in https://attestplane.io/docs/policy/forbidden_claims.md.
